resources.description:
This data has been taken from LGInform http://lginform.local.gov.uk/ the data set ref ID 206. It shows the total expenditure for Highways and transport services (RSX) in Plymouth. This data has been taken from LGInform at http://lginform.local.gov.uk/ Data set reference ID:206 The data shows the total expenditure - Highways and transport services (RSX) in Plymouth. 

This is total revenue expenditure and includes employee costs and running expenses. It is taken from the Revenue Outturn Service Expenditure Summary (RSX). 

The data are revenue outturn estimates of local authority revenue expenditure. 

These estimates are on a non-Financial Reporting Standard 17 (FRS17) and PFI “Off Balance Sheet”. 

Source name: Communities and Local Government Collection name: Revenue Outturn (RSX)

Polarity: No polarity

Polarity is how sentiment is measured "Sentiment is usually considered to have "poles" positive and negative these are often translated into "good" and "bad" sentiment analysis is considered useful to tell us what is good and bad in our information stream

  
This is the total revenue expenditure and includes employee costs and running expenses. It is taken from the Revenue Outturn Service Expenditure Summary (RSX). The data are revenue outturn estimates of local authority revenue expenditure. 

These estimates are on a non-Financial Reporting Standard 17 (FRS17) and PFI “Off Balance Sheet”. 
Source name: Communities and Local Government
Collection name: Revenue Outturn (RSX)
Polarity: No polarity
resources.name: total highways and transport expenditure

results.name: public-transport-journeys-by-type-of-transport1
results.notes: Number of journeys on the public transport network by TFL reporting period, by type of transport. Data is broken down by bus, underground, DLR, tram, Overground and cable car.Period lengths are different in periods 1 and 13, and the data is not adjusted to account for that.Docklands Light Railway journeys are based on automatic passenger counts at stations.Overground and Tram journeys are based on automatic on-carriage passenger counts.Reliable Overground journey numbers have only been available since October 2010.The IFS cloud cable car service began 28 June 2012. Weekly passenger statistics are available from the TFL website.
results.title: Public Transport Journeys by Type of Transport

results.name: transport-statistics-greater-manchester-2013-public-transport-section
results.notes: This report presents results of HFAS’s (Transport for Greater Manchester Highways Forecasting and Analytical Services formerly known as GMTU) public transport monitoring.  It includes trends in countywide rail and Metrolink patronage.

results.name: osni-open-data-50k-transport-transport-lines7
results.notes:
OSNI Open Data 50k Transport Lines. The transport layer contains all motorways, A, B, C class
and minor roads in Northern Ireland. OSNI 1:50,000 transport layer is a vector dataset providing
information on roads, road names and railways for Northern Ireland. By download or use of this
dataset you agree to abide by the LPS Open Government Data License.

organization.name: open-data-ni
organization.title: OpenDataNI
organization.description:
[OpenDataNI] (https://www.opendatani.gov.uk) is the brand name for the Open Data portal which facilitates easy access to Northern Ireland public sector data for both reuse and redistribution. 

OpenDataNI has been developed as part of the Digital NI initiative. Digital NI aims to help establish a culture within Northern Ireland that develops and enhances Digital capability not just through interactions with Government, but also with local companies and throughout communities. Within government the Digital Transformation Service is facilitating this cultural shift through a number of methods, transforming Government services, making data open and available and encouraging and coaching those who lack digital skills.

It is recognised that opening up our data is the right thing to do in terms of transparency, accountability and efficiency of the Public Sector in Northern Ireland. It has the potential to encourage new commercial opportunities and drive economic growth and innovation across public and private sectors.

results.title: OSNI Open Data - 50K Transport - Transport lines
